nodes.title: Archive Warrant Run
nodes.body: The archive keeper does not resist the warrant. He resists the pace. Every registry you ask for creates another way to lose the night, unless you can chain the documents together faster than the bank can move its own answer into the file.

choices.text: Chain the warrants and force the archive to answer as one system.

nodes.title: Fail-Forward Records
nodes.body: Three ledgers later, the archive gives way. The warehouse is no longer rumor. It is an address, a delivery window, and a signature chain pointing back to Galdermann's side of the case.

choices.text: Lock the warrant package and move on the warehouse.

nodes.title: Official Entry Ready
nodes.body: The paperwork is finally sharp enough to cut with. You can hit the warehouse in daylight and call it lawful when the shouting starts.

nodes.title: Bankhaus J.A. Krebs
nodes.body: Cold air clings to the bank's marble steps. Clara von Altenburg intercepts you before the porter can lie about how calm the building is. Inside, the hall is too orderly for a clean robbery and too frightened for an ordinary one.

choices.text: Bring Clara with you and let the room react to it.
Go in alone and keep Clara watching the floor.

nodes.title: Director Galdermann
nodes.body: Heinrich Galdermann smiles like a man used to restructuring other people's panic. He calls the vault opening an unfortunate internal matter, pushes suspicion toward his clerks, and goes briefly rigid when you mention Hartmann by name.

choices.text: Press him on Hartmann and the sealed statements.
Let the director talk and move to the clerk before he resets the story.

nodes.title: Ernst Vogel
nodes.body: Ernst Vogel looks terrified in the precise way innocence and coercion sometimes overlap. He swears the vault was locked, admits Hartmann had the kind of access no junior clerk should question, and finally blurts out Gustav the canal cleaner saw a black silhouette by the bank before dawn.

choices.text: Read the fear, not the script.
Take what he gave you and go straight to the vault.

nodes.title: Vault Inspection
nodes.body: The vault door hangs open without any sign of brute force. Dust, velvet, and chemical grit tell three different stories, which is exactly what convinces you they belong to the same job.

choices.text: Work the lock and catalogue the insider traces.
Trust the air and the chemical wrongness in the room.

nodes.title: Three Open Leads
nodes.body: Clara lays the structure out cleanly. Velvet points toward a tailor and disguises. Powder points toward a pharmacy and a supply line. Gustav's silhouette points toward a tavern and the night traffic that fed the bank. Freiburg stops being one crime scene and becomes a city of linked lies.

nodes.title: Scene: Convergence Gate
nodes.body: With at least two bundles locked, the case stops being a hunt for fragments and becomes a choice of pressure. You can force the records open through the Rathaus, or move through the workers and shadow traffic before the official story catches up.

choices.text: Commit to the official route through the Rathaus.
Commit to the covert route through the workers' channel.

nodes.title: Scene: Official Route Set
nodes.body: You choose paper, seals, and public leverage. The next move will be legal on its face and expensive in every other sense.

nodes.title: Scene: Covert Route Set
nodes.body: You choose informants, timing, and deniable access. The next move will be faster, dirtier, and much harder to explain afterward.

nodes.title: Scene: Estate Ledger
nodes.body: The estate is not haunted. It is staged. Someone used a private villa outside the main routes to store telegraph copies, costume receipts, and a payment log written in the careful half-code of people who expected the clerk to die before the archive survived.

choices.text: Take rubbings of the bureau ledger and keep the route off the official sheet.

nodes.title: Scene: Bureau Thread Confirmed
nodes.body: The ledger does not name the organization outright, but it names enough participants to prove the bank theft was cover for a bureau-grade transfer network. The case is suddenly larger than the man who will wear it in public.

nodes.title: Approach by rail
nodes.body:
**[Элеонора]**:
— Что ж, Феликс. Фрайбург не терпит опозданий. Детектив, присмотрите за ним. Он склонен теряться в… деталях, забывая о главном.

**[Лотте]**:
— На перроне лучше не останавливаться, детектив. Здесь даже прощания занимают очередь.

**[Narrator]**:
Они уходят в толпу — Элеонора и Лотте, плечом к плечу, негромко разговаривая. Рыжие волосы Лотте — последнее яркое пятно в сером паре перрона. За ними остаётся запах дорогого табака и след невысказанных обещаний.

nodes.title: Orders from the Agency
nodes.body:
Dear detective.

I await your swift arrival in Freiburg. I trust your talent shall reveal the truth behind the bank robbery. Your quarters at 'Zum Goldenen Adler' are prepared.

But remember... the most obvious path often leads to a dead end.

With respect,
Master

nodes.title: Journey to Freiburg
nodes.body:
**[Narrator]**:
Дверь в купе открывается со скрипом, и в проеме возникает высокая фигура с выдающимися скулами, из-за которых вошедший казался намного старше своих лет.

**[Assistant]**:
— Сэр, я проверил во время остановки: в газетах пусто, по радио тоже тишина.

**[inner_cynic]**:
Тишина — это не отсутствие звука. Это присутствие чьей-то очень дорогой воли.

nodes.title: Journey to Freiburg
nodes.body:
**[Assistant]**:
— Вы уверены, что это не розыгрыш?

**[inspector]**:
— Письмо было доставлено частной службой, а бумага и чернила, используемые в нем, стоят недешево. Что ж, узнаем по прибытии в отель.

**[Assistant]**:
— Вы правы, сэр. Не стали бы они арендовать нам номер просто так.

nodes.title: Journey to Freiburg
nodes.body:
**[Assistant]**:
— Мы скоро прибудем на место. Я схожу в вагон-ресторан за матушкой.

choices.text: Wait for me! I've worked up an appetite�I need a bite to eat.

nodes.title: Journey to Freiburg
nodes.body:
**[Narrator]**:
A short nod. The door slams shut. The rest of the journey you spend in the company of the letter and your growing distrust.

nodes.title: Journey to Freiburg
nodes.body:
**[Narrator]**:
The dining car greets you with the chime of crystal and the scent of expensive tobacco. Felix leads the way with confidence through the rows of tables.

**[Assistant]**:
� Mother always finds company, even on a train. It seems she's already made an acquaintance. Try to be... indulgent with her directness.

nodes.title: Journey to Freiburg
nodes.body:
**[Narrator]**:
За угловым столиком расположилась матушка Феликса. Она неторопливо потягивала белое вино, внимательно слушая свою спутницу — девушку с ярко-рыжими волосами, которая что-то оживленно рассказывала, активно жестикулируя. Огненный цвет ее волос казался вызывающе ярким в приглушенном утреннем свете вагона.

**[Redhead]**:
— ...и этот чиновник всерьез грозился засадить телеграфную службу, потому что точки в его депеше показались ему «недостаточно почтительными»... Это же просто смешно... Элеонора?

**[Assistant]**:
— Матушка, мы решили выпить перед прибытием. Не представите нас вашей спутнице?

nodes.title: Journey to Freiburg
nodes.body:
**[Элеонора]**:
— Разумеется. Лотте Ребер. О Фрайбурге она знает улицы, людей и такие двери, которые приличные дома предпочитают не замечать. А это — мой сын Феликс Хартманн. Он смотрит в окно, когда хочет, чтобы разговор обошелся без него.

**[Лотте]**:
— Элеонора делает из меня почти учреждение. Я всего лишь запоминаю, куда люди торопятся, когда уверяют, что просто гуляют.

choices.text: � Pardon the interruption�since you know the city so well, have you heard of the Zum Goldenen Adler hotel?

nodes.title: Journey to Freiburg
nodes.body:
**[Assistant]**:
— Извините. Это детектив [Name]. Он помогает нам с переездом.

**[Narrator]**:
Феликс произносит «нам» без всякого тепла, но вовремя: неловкость успевает стать его, а не вашей.

**[Лотте]**:
— Тогда будем знакомы. Люди, которые умеют молчать за столом, во Фрайбурге долго не останутся незамеченными.

nodes.title: Journey to Freiburg
nodes.body:
**[Detective]**:
— Разрешите представиться. Детектив [Name]. Прибыл во Фрайбург по делу.

**[Narrator]**:
Элеонора повторяет ваше имя беззвучно, одними губами, будто примеряет его к будущей карточке на столе.

**[Лотте]**:
— Лотте Ребер. Рада встрече, детектив. По делу — тоже, раз уж оно привело вас в наш вагон.

nodes.title: Journey to Freiburg
nodes.body:
**[Лотте]**:
— «Zum Goldenen Adler»? Хороший выбор. Старый камень, тяжелые портьеры, постояльцы, которым нравится, когда их не замечают.

**[Narrator]**:
Она произносит название без вопроса. Не вспоминает — сверяет.

nodes.title: Journey to Freiburg
nodes.body:
**[Элеонора]**:
— Попробуйте. Маркграфлерланд.

**[Narrator]**:
Официант берет бутылку за основание, оставляет этикетку на виду и льет медленно, тонкой ровной струйкой. В конце бутылка едва поворачивается, и скатерть остается чистой.

**[Элеонора]**:
— Не бойтесь — это не экзамен. Пока не экзамен.

**[inner_tradition]**:
Официант не спрашивает. Он знает, в чей бокал лить первым.

onSuccess.inlineText:
**[Perception — Успех]**:
Её пальцы — ухоженные, но не праздные. На безымянном — след от кольца, снятого недавно. Она привыкла управлять тем, что видят другие.

nodes.title: Journey to Freiburg
nodes.body:
**[Narrator]**:
За перегородкой щелкает микрофон. Кондуктор объявляет: до Фрайбурга двадцать минут.

**[Assistant]**:
— Двадцать минут. Пора убирать.

**[Narrator]**:
Элеонора смотрит на него поверх бокала. Не спорит.

**[Элеонора]**:
— Хорошо. Убирайте.

**[Narrator]**:
Лотте закрывает блокнот синхронно с объявлением — ни секундой раньше, ни секундой позже.

choices.text: Поддержать паузу и помочь свернуть разговор.
Поддержать паузу и помочь свернуть разговор.
Поддержать паузу и помочь свернуть разговор.

onSuccess.inlineText:
**[Empathy — Успех]**:
Он не раздражён. Он устал. Устал быть представленным как приложение к матери. Взгляд на часы — не нетерпение, а единственный приличный способ прервать разговор, который за него уже ведут.

nodes.title: Journey to Freiburg
nodes.body:
**[Narrator]**:
Она поднимается первой. Лотте убирает блокнот в карман пальто — не в сумку. Элеонора касается плеча Феликса: мимолётно, будто поправляя воротник.

**[Лотте]**:
— До встречи, [Name]. Фрайбург маленький — а имена в нём ходят быстрее людей.

**[Элеонора]**:
— Фрайбург нас ждёт. Впрочем, Фрайбург всегда ждёт.

nodes.title: Journey to Freiburg
nodes.body:
**[Narrator]**:
Она поднимается первой. Лотте убирает блокнот в карман пальто — не в сумку. Элеонора касается плеча Феликса: мимолётно, будто поправляя воротник.

**[Лотте]**:
— До встречи. Вы хороший слушатель — для детектива это редкость. Обычно они говорят, пока собеседник не сдастся.

**[Элеонора]**:
— Фрайбург нас ждёт. Впрочем, Фрайбург всегда ждёт.

nodes.title: Journey to Freiburg
nodes.body:
**[Narrator]**:
Она поднимается первой. Лотте убирает блокнот в карман пальто — не в сумку. Элеонора касается плеча Феликса: мимолётно, будто поправляя воротник.

**[Лотте]**:
— До встречи, детектив. «Zum Goldenen Adler» — хороший выбор. Если вдруг переедете, я обычно знаю раньше хозяина.

**[Элеонора]**:
— Фрайбург нас ждёт. Впрочем, Фрайбург всегда ждёт.

nodes.title: Journey to Freiburg
nodes.body:
**[Narrator]**:
Вы успеваете увидеть страницу: не фразы, а столбик времени — 08:12, 08:27, 08:41. Одна строка зачёркнута так ровно, будто это не пометка, а отменённый маршрут.

Лотте убирает блокнот в карман пальто — не в сумку. Элеонора касается плеча Феликса: мимолётно, будто поправляя воротник.

**[Лотте]**:
— До встречи, [Name]. Фрайбург маленький — а имена в нём ходят быстрее людей.

**[Элеонора]**:
— Фрайбург нас ждёт. Впрочем, Фрайбург всегда ждёт.

nodes.title: Journey to Freiburg
nodes.body:
**[Narrator]**:
Вы успеваете увидеть страницу: не фразы, а столбик времени — 08:12, 08:27, 08:41. Одна строка зачёркнута так ровно, будто это не пометка, а отменённый маршрут.

Лотте убирает блокнот в карман пальто — не в сумку. Элеонора касается плеча Феликса: мимолётно, будто поправляя воротник.

**[Лотте]**:
— До встречи. Вы хороший слушатель — для детектива это редкость. Обычно они говорят, пока собеседник не сдастся.

**[Элеонора]**:
— Фрайбург нас ждёт. Впрочем, Фрайбург всегда ждёт.

nodes.title: Journey to Freiburg
nodes.body:
**[Narrator]**:
Вы успеваете увидеть страницу: не фразы, а столбик времени — 08:12, 08:27, 08:41. Рядом с нижней строкой стоит ваше имя, ещё без титула.

Лотте убирает блокнот в карман пальто — не в сумку. Элеонора касается плеча Феликса: мимолётно, будто поправляя воротник.

**[Лотте]**:
— До встречи, детектив. «Zum Goldenen Adler» — хороший выбор. Если вдруг переедете, я обычно знаю раньше хозяина.

**[Элеонора]**:
— Фрайбург нас ждёт. Впрочем, Фрайбург всегда ждёт.

nodes.title: Corridor Reflection
nodes.body:
**[Narrator]**:
В купе тихо. Только ритм рельсов и мысли, которые ещё не оформились в вопросы.

**[inner_intuition]**:
Три попутчика. Один обед. Достаточно ли этого, чтобы понять — стоит ли им доверять? Или правильнее — стоит ли, чтобы они начали доверять вам?

nodes.title: Corridor Reflection
nodes.body:
**[Narrator]**:
Феликс не поблагодарил. Но он заметил — это видно по тому, как он НЕ посмотрел в вашу сторону при прощании. Молчание тоже разведка. Они говорили — вы слушали. Теперь вопрос: что из услышанного пригодится.

**[inner_intuition]**:
Три попутчика. Один обед. Достаточно ли этого, чтобы понять — стоит ли им доверять? Или правильнее — стоит ли, чтобы они начали доверять вам?

nodes.title: Corridor Reflection
nodes.body:
**[Narrator]**:
Феликс не поблагодарил. Но он заметил — это видно по тому, как он НЕ посмотрел в вашу сторону при прощании.

**[inner_intuition]**:
Три попутчика. Один обед. Достаточно ли этого, чтобы понять — стоит ли им доверять? Или правильнее — стоит ли, чтобы они начали доверять вам?

nodes.title: Corridor Reflection
nodes.body:
**[Narrator]**:
Усталость Феликса бросалась в глаза. Двадцать минут до Фрайбурга — и он считает каждую.

**[inner_intuition]**:
Три попутчика. Один обед. Достаточно ли этого, чтобы понять — стоит ли им доверять? Или правильнее — стоит ли, чтобы они начали доверять вам?

nodes.title: Corridor Reflection
nodes.body:
**[Narrator]**:
Молчание тоже разведка. Они говорили — вы слушали. Теперь вопрос: что из услышанного пригодится.

**[inner_intuition]**:
Три попутчика. Один обед. Достаточно ли этого, чтобы понять — стоит ли им доверять? Или правильнее — стоит ли, чтобы они начали доверять вам?

nodes.title: Corridor Reflection
nodes.body:
**[Narrator]**:
В купе тихо. Только ритм рельсов и мысли о гостинице «Zum Goldenen Adler», которая вас ожидает.

**[inner_intuition]**:
Три попутчика. Один обед. Достаточно ли этого, чтобы понять — стоит ли им доверять? Или правильнее — стоит ли, чтобы они начали доверять вам?

nodes.title: Platform Landing
nodes.body:
**[Narrator]**:
Пар бьёт в лицо, как первое предупреждение. Вокзал Фрайбурга — не карлсруэвская элегантность, а рабочий механизм: чугунные балки, стеклянная крыша в разводах копоти, носильщики с тележками, пахнущие углём и мокрой шерстью.

Часы над перроном показывают 08:47. Город уже проснулся.

**[attr_encyclopedia]**:
Неоренессанс. Построено при расширении Баденских железных дорог. Базель в часе пути, Страсбург — в двух. Для контрабанды и побегов — идеальный узел.

onSuccess.inlineText:
**[Perception — Успех]:**
В толпе — форма. Не железнодорожная: полицейская. Кто-то ждёт, и ждёт именно вас. Рука в кармане, взгляд по перрону — ищет не вагон, а лицо.

choices.inlineText:
**[Носильщик]**:
— Добро пожаловать во Фрайбург, господин. Экипаж госпожи Хартманн ожидает у южного выхода. Позвольте ваш багаж?

**[Narrator]**:
Он кланяется — не глубоко, но с той профессиональной точностью, которая выдаёт привычку обслуживать тех, кто платит золотом.

**[Носильщик]**:
— Извозчики слева, господин. Не задерживайте проход.

**[Narrator]**:
Он проходит мимо, даже не взглянув на вас. Во Фрайбурге уважение — это валюта, которую вы ещё не заработали.

nodes.title: On the platform
nodes.body:
The platform receives you like a room that was warned in advance.

One part of your mind starts counting exits, uniforms, luggage carts, the honest geometry of escape. Another part notices the silence first: no public outrage, no raised voices, no appetite for scandal. Freiburg has decided to keep its pulse hidden.

A third, less useful but never absent, whispers that the city already knows your name and resents you for arriving late.

Good. Let it resent. Silence is still testimony, if you stand inside it long enough.

nodes.title: Parting
nodes.body:
**[Элеонора]**:
— Что ж, Феликс. Фрайбург не терпит опозданий. Детектив, присмотрите за ним. Он склонен теряться в… деталях, забывая о главном.

**[Лотте]**:
— На перроне лучше не останавливаться, детектив. Здесь даже прощания занимают очередь.

**[Narrator]**:
Они уходят в толпу — Элеонора и Лотте, плечом к плечу, негромко разговаривая. Рыжие волосы Лотте — последнее яркое пятно в сером паре перрона. За ними остаётся запах дорогого табака и след невысказанных обещаний.

nodes.title: Name in Circulation
nodes.body:
A porter checks a card before you can give your name. He does not greet you as a stranger; he makes room as if the station has already been told where to place you.

It is useful, in the way a locked door is useful when someone else holds the key.

nodes.title: Hartmann Courtesy
nodes.body:
A porter tips his cap before you ask for help. 'The Hartmann party has already passed word,' he says, and lifts your bag with the care reserved for property that belongs near influence.

The courtesy saves a minute. It also tells you whose shadow reached the platform first.

nodes.title: Hauptbahnhof, Freiburg
nodes.body:
Steam folds around the iron columns and the first rush of arriving passengers. A boy with newspapers cuts between trunks like a thought no one can pin down. Somewhere farther down the platform, metal tags knock softly against a luggage grille, and beyond that a police post studies the crowd with professional boredom.

Freiburg has not greeted you. It has merely failed to hide.

nodes.title: Evening edition
nodes.body:
The boy is nervous. He grips the thin, hurried evening edition like a shield. You notice the bank's name � Bankhaus Krebs � in the headlines. It's too early for official news, too specific for rumor.

Someone wanted this story told before the dust even settled.

choices.text: Try to get a closer look at the boy's behavior.
Just buy a paper and move on.

nodes.title: HBF Arrival
nodes.body:
As you reach for a paper, the boy's hand trembles. He's not just selling news; he's watching for someone. Your assistant leans in, a silent shadow that makes the boy stiffen further.

'Go,' you mutter. He doesn't wait for a second invitation.

nodes.title: HBF Arrival
nodes.body: You take the paper. The ink is still fresh enough to smudge your gloves. The boy scurries away into the steam without looking back.

nodes.title: Luggage Counter
nodes.body:
The clerk is counting brass tags. A heavy wooden crate marked for Bankhaus J.A. Krebs sits on a trolley, arriving from Strasbourg under priority seal. It's unusual for a local bank to receive such a delivery on a Sunday morning.

The clerk notices you lingering and shifts a clipboard to cover the manifest.

choices.text: Press the clerk about the Strasbourg shipment.

nodes.title: HBF Arrival
nodes.body:
The clerk's eyes dart toward the police post. 'I don't know anything about Strasbourg,' he mutters. 'Only that the bank requested priority. If you want to know more, go to the source.'

He turns his back on you, ending the conversation with a sharp snap of his ledger.

nodes.title: Police Post
nodes.body:
Two officers are deep in low-voiced conversation. They mention an 'open vault' and a 'silent alarm' that didn't ring. Their posture is rigid, eyes scanning the crowd with more than just regular vigilance.

They are waiting for someone. Or preventing someone from leaving.

nodes.title: Leaving the Hauptbahnhof
nodes.body:
You shoulder through the tide of travelers � timetables, porters and polite lies that pretend to be small talk.

The glass doors spill you into Freiburg. Two fronts are burning: the bank robbery and the political pressure from the Rathaus. Where you go first will shape how the city sees you.

choices.text: The bank first. Follow the money.
The Rathaus first. Follow the power.

nodes.title: Freiburg
nodes.body: The station is behind you. The city is ahead.

nodes.title: Scene: Lowen Apotheke
nodes.body: The apothecary does not deny the compound once you name its smell. The residue is real, the purchase route runs through university stock, and the order was signed under cover of a sender name no honest clerk would trust twice.

choices.text: Record the formula trail and move the chemical bundle forward.

nodes.title: Scene: Chemical Bundle Locked
nodes.body: By the time you leave, the residue is no longer mysterious. It is logistical, expensive, and routed through people who expected the chemistry to look more important than the theft.

nodes.title: Scene: Zum Schlappen
nodes.body: The tavern keeper watches the room before answering. Once Gustav Brandt realizes you can offer protection instead of theatre, he confirms Hartmann's name, a warehouse window, and a disguised runner moving under worker cover after curfew.

choices.text: Take Gustav's timing window and close the logistics bundle.

nodes.title: Scene: Logistics Bundle Locked
nodes.body: You leave the pub with the first route to the warehouse that sounds like a schedule instead of a rumor. Somebody is moving people, ledgers, and disguises on the same night rhythm.

nodes.title: Scene: Tailor Workshop
nodes.body: Herr Klein recognizes the torn velvet before he finishes pretending it is ordinary stage cloth. Hartmann paid for a disguise runner, Box 217 stored the costume, and somebody with bank access wanted to walk through Freiburg wearing someone else's class.

choices.text: Take the costume ledger copy and lock the identity trail.

nodes.title: Scene: Identity Bundle Locked
nodes.body: The tailor does not want his name in the file. You do not need it there yet. What matters is the route: disguise, cash runner, Hartmann.

nodes.title: Scene: Zum Goldenen Adler
nodes.body: The inn keeps its warmth behind polished wood and practiced discretion. Your room is reserved, your name is legible in the register, and the clerk has already decided which parts of your arrival are ordinary enough to say aloud.

choices.text: Ask why the route was ready before you arrived.
Take the room and keep the inn out of the file for now.

nodes.title: Scene: Route Already Marked
nodes.body: The clerk lowers his eyes to the register. "Fraulein Weber asked whether the room would be aired before the noon rush. She did not ask twice." On the blotter lies a timetable corner, folded once. 08:41 is underlined; not in ink, but by pressure. Freiburg has not followed you. ...

choices.text: Leave the timetable where it is and take the key.

nodes.title: Scene: Key Taken
nodes.body: The key is plain brass, heavier than it looks. Whatever else Zum Goldenen Adler knows, it can wait behind a locked door while the city begins to spend its morning.

nodes.title: Scene: Lotte on the Wire
nodes.body: The telephone line hisses before Lotte Weber speaks. She has seen switchboard traffic redirect itself around your questions, which means somebody knows the investigation is narrowing. Her warning is plain: if you keep pulling the thread in daylight, the city will pull back in ...

choices.text: Ask why her train notes kept time instead of names.
Let the silence do some of the work before you answer.
Thank her and ask for one more quiet relay.
Keep it professional and tell her to stay off the record.

nodes.title: Scene: Time on the Wire
nodes.body: **[Detective]**: -- In the train, you were not taking notes. You were keeping time. **[Lotte]**: -- Time keeps itself. I only mark when people pretend they arrived by chance. The line hisses around her answer. She does not deny the schedule; she only waits to see whether you u...

choices.text: Use the timing and ask for one more quiet relay.
Keep the timing off the record and set a boundary.

nodes.title: Scene: Listener on the Wire
nodes.body: You do not fill the hiss with a question. For three seconds the line carries only the room around her: switchboard clicks, paper shifting, one careful breath. "Still listening," Lotte says. "Good. Most men only pause long enough to reload their own certainty." The warning has ...

choices.text: Keep the line open and ask for one more quiet relay.
Keep the silence professional and tell her to stay off the record.

nodes.title: Scene: Channel Preserved
nodes.body: Lotte does not soften, but she does stay on the line. The warning becomes a working channel instead of a courtesy. She is trusting you with more than a message now.

nodes.title: Scene: Channel Narrowed
nodes.body: She accepts the distance faster than you wanted her to. The warning stands, but the next call will cost more trust than this one did.

nodes.title: Scene: Mayor's Office
nodes.body: The mayor leaves you standing long enough to make the room feel like a test. He wants the panic contained, the council reassured, and the bank matter closed before the newspapers decide City Hall was in on it. The message is polite only because there are clerks listening outsi...

choices.text: Let the Rathaus note that you arrived without Hartmann sponsorship.
Ask what the Rathaus is most afraid of.
Ask why Clara von Altenburg is already moving around the bank.

nodes.title: Scene: Independent Footing
nodes.body: The mayor's secretary notices the absence before the mayor admits it. No Hartmann carriage waits outside, no borrowed calling card lies on the tray, no soft introduction has crossed the desk ahead of you. "Good," the mayor says at last. "Then for the next few minutes this can ...

choices.text: Take the cleaner footing and ask what the Rathaus is most afraid of.

nodes.title: Scene: Political Pressure
nodes.body: He gives you three things and pretends they are one: a permit to press deeper into the records later, a warning that Galdermann has friends who pay for silence, and a refusal to say Hartmann's name until you give him something the council can survive hearing in public.

choices.text: Let Felix read the official cover before you accept it.
Take the briefing and move to the bank with official cover.

nodes.title: Scene: Felix Reads the Cover
nodes.body: Felix takes the permit as if it might bruise. He reads the mayor's phrasing twice, once for law and once for cowardice. "This gives you doors," he says quietly. "Not protection. If the Rathaus needs distance later, every sentence here already knows how to step away from you." ...

choices.text: Take Felix's reading and move to the bank with official cover.

nodes.title: Scene: Official Writ
nodes.body: By the time you leave, you have enough paper to open doors and enough political pressure to know those same doors may close behind you.

nodes.title: Rail Yard Tail
nodes.body: The yard is moving even before midnight. Workers who never saw you before decide not to remember you, and that is the best kind of permission you are going to get on this route.

choices.text: Blend with the shift change and follow the shadow traffic.

nodes.title: Covert Entry Window
nodes.body: The tail confirms what the pub only suggested: the warehouse is live, the guards are being rotated, and one quiet breach is still possible before the ledgers leave the district.

choices.text: Keep the route quiet and move before dawn.

nodes.title: Covert Entry Ready
nodes.body: You now have a real breach window. It will never look clean, but it will get you inside before the official story can catch the evidence.

nodes.title: Scene: Warehouse Door
nodes.body: The warehouse smells of wet timber, ledger ink, and a job that expected to end before witnesses arrived. Galdermann is not alone, but he is the one who understands what the room means if you leave with the right papers.

choices.text: Seal the floor, call the warrant, and force a lawful close.
Use the bureau ledger and force a compromised truth instead of a public one.

nodes.title: Scene: Lawful Close
nodes.body: Galdermann folds when the warrant lands and the archive chain holds. The case closes in public, the network survives offstage, and the University thread is all that remains obvious enough to chase next.

nodes.title: Scene: Compromised Truth
nodes.body: You close the visible case, but only by leaving the bureau thread alive enough to watch. Galdermann becomes the public culprit, the network goes to ground, and the University connection is now the only doorway that still opens forward.

nodes.title: Scene: Municipal Archive
nodes.body: Dust hangs in thin vertical shafts of light. Archivist Otto taps a stack of index cards and says, "Missing pages are louder than complete ones, if you know where to listen."

choices.text: [Intellect] Rebuild the missing index from memory.
Ignore catalog policy and jump to restricted ledgers.

nodes.title: Scene: Catalog Drift
nodes.body: The catalog numbers jump by two, then by nine, then by one again. Administrative chaos would be random; this pattern is curated.

choices.text: Flag irregular signatures for later comparison.

nodes.title: Scene: Ledger Discrepancy
nodes.body: A municipal tax ledger references a bank annex that does not exist on any public map. Otto does not seem surprised. He seems annoyed that you noticed.

choices.text: Cross-link the missing folios to municipal tax seals.
[Flaw] Rewrite the archive index in your own notation.

nodes.title: Scene: Restricted Drawer
nodes.body: A restricted drawer opens with a reluctant click. Inside: one index card, one date, and one routing code that points directly at the bank's sealed archive wing.

choices.text: Keep the card hidden and request one final key.

nodes.title: Scene: Indexed Key
nodes.body: Otto signs the release registry with visible irritation and leaves the key in your line of sight. "Catalog it before you use it," he says. "Order first, panic later."

choices.text: Catalog the key and leave before dawn.

nodes.title: Scene: Exit from Archives
nodes.body: You leave with card edges still biting your fingertips. The city above ground feels less stable now that its records no longer agree with themselves.

nodes.title: Scene: Baroness in the Winter Salon
nodes.body: The salon is warm with coal heat and colder with protocol. Baroness Elise waits by the marble fireplace with a ledger tied in blue ribbon. "If you are here to investigate, inspector, you must first decide whether you still understand manners."

choices.text: Skip formalities and ask about the debt register.

nodes.title: Scene: Ballroom Ledger
nodes.body: A quartet plays somewhere behind closed doors while Elise opens the ledger to a page of carefully erased names. Every blank line is expensive. Every correction looks intentional.

choices.text: Accept a private lead ledger from the Baroness.

nodes.title: Scene: Debt Registry
nodes.body: The debt registry is a machine of social blackmail. Initials, dates, signatures, and one column marked "informal obligation" in red ink. The Baroness watches your eyes more than your hands.

choices.text: Trace signatures from the unpaid pages.
[Flaw] Publicly humiliate a minor lord to force compliance.

nodes.title: Scene: Letter of Compromise
nodes.body: A folded letter sealed with a noble crest slides across the table. "A banker signed this before he denied everything," Elise says. "Use it carefully. Or use it brutally."

choices.text: Lock the compromise letter in your coat.

nodes.title: Scene: Favor Contract
nodes.body: You negotiate without writing anything down. The terms are obvious anyway: she gets timing control, you get access, and both of you pretend this is temporary.

choices.text: Promise one favor in exchange for silence.

nodes.title: Scene: Exit from the Salon
nodes.body: Outside, carriage wheels hiss across wet stone. You leave with a seal, a favor, and the certainty that etiquette is just another weapon when used correctly.

nodes.title: Сцена: Cafe Riegler
nodes.body: A silver teaspoon clinks sparsely against fine porcelain. Cafe Riegler smells faintly of almond cake and old, damp wool. Through the window, the Schwabentor looms, indifferent to the gray afternoon. Anna Mahler sits opposite you. She looks tired, though her posture remains imp...

choices.text: [Shivers] Close your eyes and feel the vibration of the floor.
"A detective only digs when the client pays for the shovel, Anna."

nodes.title: Сцена: Shivers
nodes.body: SHIVERS [Medium: Success] - It begins as a faint thrumming against the soles of your shoes. The ancient cobblestones of Freiburg are murmuring. An odd, melancholy vibration—like the distant tolling of the Münster bells, but coming from deep within the earth. A secret is gestat...

choices.text: She looks at you like you've grown a second head.

nodes.title: Сцена: Anna's Secret
nodes.body: Anna’s eyes dart toward the door before returning to her cooling tea. "There is a ledger at the Provincial Bank. It is heavy, bound in black leather," she murmurs, her voice barely carrying over the scrape of chairs. "But it is what is absent that matters. Blank pages where th...

choices.text: A messenger boy in a tattered blue cap skids to a halt by your table.
[Flaw] Bet your last coin on the messenger's destination.

nodes.title: Сцена: The Messenger
nodes.body: PERCEPTION [Easy: Success] - The brass bell above the cafe door shivers. A boy in a damp coat stands dripping in the entryway. He deposits an envelope on the table. It bears no name, only a crimson wax seal pressed with the heavy, unblinking crest of the Provincial Bank. The s...

choices.text: "It seems an exclusive awaits us. But keep your mouth shut."
Pocket the telegram without a word.
[Flaw] Offer the boy double if he tells who paid him.

nodes.title: Сцена: Show Telegram
nodes.body: DRAMA [Medium: Success] - The red wax sits between you like a drop of spilled blood. Anna traces the rim of her cup. "A summons from the crypt," she says, a flicker of genuine apprehension crossing her otherwise placid face. "I had hoped it was merely a rumor. It seems the gho...

choices.text: Anna's smirk softens into a look of genuine intrigue.

nodes.title: Сцена: Anna's Tip
nodes.body: "If the dead are waking, you will need more than a press pass." She slides a heavy brass key across the table, hiding it beneath the financial section of a discarded newspaper. It is cold, jagged, and smells faintly of ozone and old iron. "A cast of the archive door. Do not li...

choices.text: You drain the last of your cold coffee and stand up.

nodes.title: Сцена: Exit
nodes.body: The tea has grown entirely cold. INLAND EMPIRE [Formidable: Success] - The brass key rests heavily in your coat pocket. Outside, the rain begins to fall with a rhythmic, mournful persistence. The narrow streets of Freiburg stretch out like veins leading back to the heart of a ...

nodes.title: Scene: Muster Yard
nodes.body: Rain drums on the corrugated roof above the muster yard. Major Falk stands beside a rusted field map. "Old wars never end," he says, "they just rent rooms in your head."

choices.text: [Spirit] Hold your stance while artillery memories return.
Push the memories down and request immediate briefing.

nodes.title: Scene: Artillery Echo
nodes.body: For a second the rain is artillery and the yard is a trench. Then it passes. Your pulse settles enough to keep listening.

choices.text: Count breathing cadence until your hands stop shaking.

nodes.title: Scene: Dispatch Envelope
nodes.body: Falk hands you a dispatch stamped with military inventory codes. The route intersects civilian banking traffic three times in one night. That is not an accident.

choices.text: Memorize the courier route and unit aliases.
[Flaw] Lash out at a trainee who dropped his rifle.

nodes.title: Scene: Checkpoint Memories
nodes.body: A temporary checkpoint sits where no official map shows one. You can still read the old routines: who salutes, who hesitates, who lies by overexplaining.

choices.text: Call in old favors to pass an inspection line.

nodes.title: Scene: Quiet Oath
nodes.body: You and Falk speak under the awning while recruits pretend not to listen. No speeches. Just one promise that this case will not become another buried report.

choices.text: Swear to finish what your unit never could.

nodes.title: Scene: Exit from Barracks
nodes.body: The rain thins out as you step into the street. Your coat smells of oil and wet canvas, and your pocket is heavier with official paper than it should be.

nodes.title: Immersion Bath
nodes.body: Steam claws at your lungs as Lotte Weber orders you upright from the Agency immersion tub before the memory fog can settle.

choices.text: Grip the tub rim and force yourself upright.

nodes.title: Missing Notes
nodes.body: Your satchel is empty, your notes are gone, and Weber refuses to explain until you prove you can still think on your feet.

choices.text: Ask Lotte where you are and why your notes are gone.
Ignore the answer for a second and inspect the steam valve.

nodes.title: Steam Valve
nodes.body: The valve hisses beside the tub, wet metal and rushed maintenance turning the cellar into a machine room that never quite cools.

choices.text: Trace the wet pipe to the ledger crate by the wall.
Leave the hardware alone and face Lotte properly.

nodes.title: Ledger Crate
nodes.body: A swollen ledger lies beside the wall, its page edges marked by recent handling and the kind of haste that leaves trails.

choices.text: Set the ledger down and inspect the drying uniform.
Keep the page numbers in mind and hear the offer out.

nodes.title: Drying Uniform
nodes.body: A fresh inspector's coat hangs above the boiler, still damp from the bath heat and already waiting to become your cover.

choices.text: Pull the coat on and demand the short version.

nodes.title: Weber's Pitch
nodes.body: Weber drops the performance at last: the Agency needs a journalist who can wear a badge, follow the money, and survive the city long enough to report back.

nodes.title: Karlsruhe Hauptbahnhof
nodes.body: Steam from the first morning train hangs over the platform. Victoria Sterling spots you before the porters do, already carrying a folded dispatch case and a list of unfinished meetings.

choices.text: Take pace beside Victoria and ask for the situation.

nodes.title: Morning Edition
nodes.body: Before Victoria can finish her briefing, a paperboy wedges himself between you with today's edition. The headline names three embarrassments before breakfast: the bank robbery, the mayor's vanished dog, and a bakery whose signature scent has disappeared overnight.

choices.text: Buy the paper and keep him talking.
Skim the front page and wave him on.

nodes.title: Victoria's Briefing
nodes.body: Victoria taps the paper with one gloved finger. 'Three public fires, one morning. We do not have time for ceremony. Take the city as it is, not as Freiburg trained you to expect it.'

choices.text: Take the newspaper and move out.

nodes.title: Case Ledger Opened
nodes.body: The folded newspaper becomes your first Karlsruhe lead sheet. Bank. Rathaus. Bakery. Three names, no ceremony, and the city already moving before you choose.

nodes.title: Entry: scene_backstory_select
nodes.body: Preparing investigation dossier...

nodes.title: Entry: scene_backstory_select
nodes.body: Preparing case file...

nodes.title: Agency Briefing
nodes.body: The agency shutters are drawn. A fresh case board waits under gaslight, and the city outside is still mostly blank to you. The inspector slides a folder across the desk: the bank, the cathedral square, the worker taverns. Freiburg only starts to make sense once you know where ...

choices.text: Take the first assignments and open the city map.

nodes.title: The City Opens
nodes.body: Pins begin to surface on the agency map. The obvious landmarks appear first, but the darker routes are still submerged beneath the surface. Your first assignment is clear: start with the bank and follow whatever falls out of the ledger.

nodes.title: Agency Promotion Review
nodes.body: The agency board finally reflects that the banker file, the rumor chain, and the source work all landed on one record.

choices.text: File the promotion review and return to operations.

nodes.title: Promotion Filed
nodes.body: The promotion file closes cleanly, and the next Freiburg route now sees the rank on your name.

nodes.title: Agency Service: Anna's Introduction
nodes.body: Anna can still open the student house, but only if your ledger with her or the agency still carries enough weight.

choices.text: Commit Anna's introduction to the banker file.
Hold the introduction in reserve for now.

nodes.title: Agency Desk
nodes.body: The file returns to the board with Anna's channels either committed or kept in reserve.

nodes.title: Сцена: Разговор с банкиром
nodes.body: - Вход из [[40_GameViewer/Sandbox_KA/02_Quest_Entries/scene_banker_entry|scene_banker_entry]]. - `sandbox_banker` в статусе `started`. - Визуал: кабинет с закрытым сейфом, ведомости на столе.

nodes.title: Choice: Принять дело без конфронтации
nodes.body: - `choice_id`: `BANK_INTRO_ACCEPT` - `gating_check`: none - `success_effect`: открывает хаб следов

nodes.title: Choice: Надавить на мотивы клиента
nodes.body: - `choice_id`: `BANK_INTRO_PRESS_MOTIVE` - `gating_check`: `logic >= 2` or `empathy >= 2` - `success_effect`: `clue_CLUE_B05_WAX_ON_GLOVE = true`

nodes.title: Сцена-хаб: Следы по делу Вагнера
nodes.body: - Вход из `scene_bank_intro_ch1` или `scene_bank_intro_ch2`. - Повторный вход из `scene_son_house` и `scene_tavern`. - `sandbox_banker = started`

nodes.title: Сцена: Казино и очная ставка
nodes.body: - Вход из [[40_GameViewer/Sandbox_KA/Plot/01_Banker/scene_bank_leads|scene_bank_leads]]. - `sandbox_banker = started` - Рекомендуется хотя бы одна собранная улика (`CLUE_B01` или `CLUE_B03`), но вход не блокируется.

nodes.title: Сцена: Казино и очная ставка
nodes.body: - Вход из [[40_GameViewer/Sandbox_KA/Plot/01_Banker/scene_bank_leads|scene_bank_leads]]. - `sandbox_banker = started` - Рекомендуется хотя бы одна собранная улика (`CLUE_B01` или `CLUE_B03`), но вход не блокируется.

nodes.title: Сцена: Казино и очная ставка
nodes.body: - Вход из [[40_GameViewer/Sandbox_KA/Plot/01_Banker/scene_bank_leads|scene_bank_leads]]. - `sandbox_banker = started` - Рекомендуется хотя бы одна собранная улика (`CLUE_B01` или `CLUE_B03`), но вход не блокируется.

nodes.title: Journey to Freiburg
nodes.body:
**[Narrator]**:
"Карлсруэ смыло дождем. В купе пахнет пылью и остывшим углем."


**[inner_cynic]**:
"Семь слов в кармане. Семь слов, чтобы ты окончательно сошел с ума."


**[inner_analyst]**:
"Имперский Банк. Это не грабеж, это вызов геометрии порядка."

**[Narrator]**:
"Скрип железа. Туман за стеклом слишком плотный для обычного утра.

Проводник объявляет: _«Nächster Halt — Freiburg im Breisgau!»_"


**[inner_exile]**:
"Фрайбург. Город, который не ждал тебя, но уже подготовил камеру."


**[Narrator]**:
"Перрон. Неоренессанс в копоти. Ты здесь."

choices.text: Follow Felix to the dining car.

nodes.title: Dining Car — Eleonora, Lotte & Wine
nodes.body:
Detective follows Felix to the dining car. Eleonora and Lotte are already seated at a corner table, wine and tea between them. The scene establishes:

1. **Eleonora's control** — how she manages space, attention, and social architecture
2. **Lotte's dual identity** — journalist tells (notebook, pen callus) visible to perceptive players
3. **Felix's fatigue** — the first signs of the apathy mechanic (being presented as an appendage)
4. **Player agency** — wine acceptance sets `flag_joked_with_mother`, Felix defense sets `flag_defended_felix`

nodes.title: HBF Arrival
nodes.body:
**[Narrator]**:
"Поезд останавливается с пронзительным скрежетом тормозов. [clue:Пар:ev_station_steam] заполняет платформу,
скрывая очертания вокзала. Воздух пахнет [clue:угольным дымом:ev_coal_smell] и сырым камнем.

Ты выходишь из вагона, чувствуя [clue:утренний холод:ev_morning_chill] сквозь пальто. Толпа пассажиров
устремляется к выходу — [clue:шляпы, чемоданы, зонты:ev_rushing_crowd]. Никто не обращает на тебя внимания.

Где-то впереди — Фрайбург. И дело, ради которого ты здесь."

**Passive Perception (DC 7)**

- Success: You identify Fritz early in the moving crowd.
- Failure: You only see crowd noise and must locate him through action.

After the check, the station pressure remains high and the player must decide how to proceed.

nodes.title: Choice: Approach the Uniform
nodes.body:
You move straight through the foot traffic toward the uniformed officer before
the station can swallow the moment.

nodes.title: Choice: Investigate Station
nodes.body:
Instead of closing distance, you let the platform speak first and map the flow
of people, voices, and nervous movement.

nodes.title: Meeting Fritz (Direct)
nodes.body:
You intercept Fritz before he finishes shouting to the constables. He takes one
look at your badge and switches to mission mode.

choices.text: Ask for the formal mission briefing.

nodes.title: Action: Investigate Station
nodes.body:
You delay contact with Fritz and test the platform for weak points. A paperboy
passes too close, and the station reveals three immediate options.

choices.text: Glance at the headline without paying.
Move on and skip the kiosk.

nodes.title: Decision: Set Priority
nodes.body:
Fritz lays out two urgent fronts: the bank robbery and the political pressure
from Rathaus. You choose what burns first.

nodes.title: Meeting Fritz (Indirect)
nodes.body:
Fritz notices that you took the long way around and reads it instantly. He does
not approve of delays, but he respects useful observation.

nodes.title: Scene: Paperboy Theft Attempt
nodes.body:
The boy bumps your shoulder and reaches for your watch in one smooth motion.
You catch the wrist, and Fritz arrives before the crowd can react.

choices.text: Decide what happens to the paperboy.

nodes.title: Map: Freiburg Transit
nodes.body:
The city map opens with two immediate routes. The next phase starts once you
pick your first destination.

nodes.title: Choice: Paperboy Fate
nodes.body: Fritz waits for your ruling while the boy twists in your grip.

choices.text: Show mercy and let him run.

nodes.title: Case01 Onboarding Handoff
nodes.body: Onboarding pilot is complete. Continue into the next Case01 phase.

nodes.title: City Bootblack Tip
nodes.body: The bootblack shares a quiet rumor about the streets.

nodes.title: Bootblack Tip End
nodes.body: You flip him a coin and walk away.

nodes.title: City Cleaner Tip
nodes.body: The cleaner mutters something about strange hours at the banks.

nodes.title: Cleaner Tip End
nodes.body: You nod slowly, taking note.

nodes.title: City Student Tip
nodes.body: A student drops their books and complains about the university curfews.

nodes.title: Student Tip End
nodes.body: You offer a sympathetic hum.

nodes.title: Scene: Detective Agency Wakeup
nodes.body: Cold window light cuts across the desk before the coffee does. The Freiburg file is already open, weighted down by a brass ruler and the night's unanswered telegrams.

nodes.title: Scene: Agency Orientation
nodes.body: Victoria Sterling does not waste your time with pleasantries. She pushes the bank dossier across the table, already sorted into witness names, timings, and the first contradictions.

choices.text: Press Victoria for the missing witness names

nodes.title: Scene: Bank Arrival
nodes.body: The iron gate is still warm from the first police shift. Inside, the service corridor smells of polish, steam, and a fear that has already learned to hide.

nodes.title: Scene: First Sweep
nodes.body: The first pass through the room is never about brilliance. It is about rhythm: door, ledger, window latch, clerk's shoes, the ash tray nobody admits to using.

nodes.title: Dog Caseboard
nodes.body: The caseboard is filled with red strings and polaroids. What does it all mean?

choices.text: Pressure the vendor for route details

nodes.title: Market Lead - Beat 2
nodes.body: The vendor seems to have nothing more to say. Perhaps you should look elsewhere.

choices.text: Bribe the porter for schedule logs

nodes.title: Station Lead - Beat 2
nodes.body: The trains come and go. You've gathered all the witness accounts you could.

nodes.title: Tailor Lead - Beat 2
nodes.body: The tailor continues to work on the fabrics, ignoring your pointed glares.

nodes.title: Pub Lead - Beat 2
nodes.body: The pub is loud and full of patrons. The trail here seems cold for now.

nodes.title: 📖 Beat 1: A Good Boy?
nodes.body: **[Narrator]**: "Under an old oak tree, you find the fugitive. Bruno lies on his back, paws in the air, sleeping off a food coma.

nodes.title: 📖 Beat 1: The Von Hagen Mystery
nodes.body: **[Narrator]**: "The von Hagen estate looms at the edge of Durlach, its windows dark like empty eye sockets. A cold wind blows through the overgrown garden, carrying the scent of dead leaves."

nodes.title: 📖 Beat 1: The Art of Deduction
nodes.body: **[Guildmaster Klaus]**: "Ah, you have gathered quite a collection of facts. But facts alone are just bricks. You must build a house with them."

nodes.title: 📖 Beat 1: The Library
nodes.body: **[Narrator]**: "The library is freezing, despite the fireplace being lit. Your breath forms clouds in the air.

nodes.title: ðŸ•µï¸ Conclusion: The Smuggler's Trick
nodes.body: **[Narrator]**: "You reveal the hidden mechanism behind the bookshelf. A draft of cold air rushes out from the tunnels below. Inside, crates of smuggled brandy."

nodes.title: ðŸ‘» Conclusion: The Supernatural Truth
nodes.body: **[Narrator]**: "You confront the Baroness with your findings. The cold spots, the ectoplasm... This is no trick. The estate is truly haunted."

nodes.title: Inner Voices Demo
nodes.body: A frightened courier offers you a ledger and begs for safe passage before the city closes around him.

choices.text: Hide the courier and slow the pursuit.

innerVoiceHints.text: Keep him alive, even if it costs initiative.
Mercy spends leverage and buys you no guarantee.

choices.text: Take the ledger and make the courier owe you.

innerVoiceHints.text: Take the asset first and keep the debt in your pocket.
Do not turn fear into tribute when trust is still possible.

nodes.title: Afterimage
nodes.body: The room quiets, but the argument inside you sharpens into two clear routes.

innerVoiceHints.text: You already chose to protect someone. Finish the line cleanly.

choices.text: Press the hard edge while it still holds.

innerVoiceHints.text: You have the leverage now. Use it before the city shifts again.

nodes.title: Shared Burden
nodes.body: You move slower, but the courier leaves alive and the city owes you a different kind of memory.

nodes.title: Cold Leverage
nodes.body: You leave with the ledger, the advantage, and a silence that will not forgive you soon.

nodes.title: Entry: scene_start
nodes.body: - Campaign starts in Karlsruhe sandbox onboarding. - No prior sandbox flags required. - Cold open with travel and office arrival tone.

nodes.title: Entry: scene_language_select
nodes.body: - Entered from [[40_GameViewer/Sandbox_KA/00_Entry/scene_start|scene_start]]. - Start scene completed. - Player chooses interface language and readability settings.

nodes.title: Entry: scene_backstory_select
nodes.body: - Entered from [[40_GameViewer/Sandbox_KA/00_Entry/scene_language_select|scene_language_select]]. - Language/config setup completed. - Select starting background flavor for roleplay framing.

nodes.title: Entry: scene_map_intro
nodes.body: - Entered from [[40_GameViewer/Sandbox_KA/00_Entry/scene_backstory_select|scene_backstory_select]]. - Entry chain is complete. - Introduces free-order sandbox structure and map-centric play.

nodes.title: Loop Demo Start
nodes.body: This is a demo scenario for testing the Mind Palace loop.

nodes.title: Loop Demo End
nodes.body: You have discovered the fact. Please check your Mind Palace.

nodes.title: Bakery Counter
nodes.body: The baker does not talk about thefts. He talks about absences. This morning the ovens worked, the bread rose, and yet the cardamom signature that draws half the street simply never arrived.

choices.text: Inspect the ovens and spice shelf.
Question the apprentice about the missing scent.

nodes.title: Cooling Rack
nodes.body: The missing aroma was not stolen from the tray. It was intercepted before the dough reached the front room. A torn paper wrap and a wet footprint point toward the service alley.

choices.text: Follow the service trail into the alley.

nodes.title: Service Alley
nodes.body: You find the missing spice packet where a courier tried to dry it over a boiler vent. The aroma was borrowed to fake a premium delivery, not to poison the batch. Sloppy, urgent, and easy to unwind.

choices.text: Recover the packet and close the file quietly.

nodes.title: Aroma Restored
nodes.body: The baker relights the oven, the scent returns to the room, and the neighborhood never learns how close it came to panic over a missing spice packet.

nodes.title: Student House Access
nodes.body: A fraternity porter weighs Anna's name against your badge before deciding whether the door opens.

choices.text: Present Anna's introduction and enter the house.

nodes.title: Back On The Street
nodes.body: You leave the student house route with its cost now written into the wider Freiburg file.

nodes.title: Tavern Whisper
nodes.body: A dockhand leans in just long enough to mutter about a street raid breaking apart near the rail yards. If you move now, there may still be witnesses to catch.

choices.text: Cut through the alleys before the lead goes cold.

nodes.title: Late to the Alley
nodes.body: By the time you arrive, the shouting has already thinned into scattered footsteps and a single overturned crate. Someone was here. Someone will talk later if you come back with better timing.

facts.text: This is a demo fact proving the loop works.
Client ledger has a deliberate accounting gap for one night.
The banker's hands trembled when discussing the ledger dates.
House steward confirms the banker met an unknown courier.
Tavern records break the client's alibi timeline.
Telegram seal matches Provincial Bank emergency dispatch wax.
Anna hands over a cast of the archive master key.
Trader confirms someone matching Dog crossed toward the station.
Night train manifests show a passenger fitting Dog's description.
Tailor's secret log shows bespoke trench coats paid by an unknown handler.
Barman described a handler meeting people looking exactly like Dog.
University registry lists a researcher missing around the time Dog appeared.
Dog intercepted you in the park, demanding answers about their origin.

hypotheses.text: The loop demo has been completed successfully.
The banker staged an inside theft and used a courier cover.
The Dog traveled from the university area via the market toward the train station.
A proxy handler operates out of the pub, outfitting assets at the tailor. Dog confronted me looking for them.

points.title: Grenzwanderer Agency
points.description: Your hidden office and operations hub. Briefings, evidence walls, and trusted informants start here.

points.title: Hauptbahnhof
points.description: Steam-era gateway to Freiburg. Arrivals from Basel and Strasbourg.

points.title: Bankhaus J.A. Krebs
points.description: Prestigious bank at Munsterplatz, currently under renovation.

points.title: Rathaus
points.description: City Hall and records authority for central Freiburg.

points.title: Freiburg Munster
points.description: Gothic cathedral dominating the city center.

points.title: Kiliani Laboratory
points.description: University chemistry lab for forensic analysis.

points.title: Institute of Hygiene
points.description: Medical institute focused on serology and lab diagnostics.

points.title: Corps Suevia House
points.description: Fraternity house in the university quarter.

points.title: Zum Goldenen Adler
points.description: Reserved inn lodging where Freiburg's first social weather starts to collect.

points.title: Gerberau Canal
points.description: Tanners quarter where movement is best watched at night.

points.title: Old Warehouse
points.description: Rail yard storage facility used for late-stage investigation.

points.title: Edge Estate
points.description: A private estate used as a quiet relay point for the case's hidden paperwork.

points.title: The Red Cog Tavern
points.description: Workers tavern and hub for social leads.

points.title: Martinstor
points.description: Historic city gate with strong foot traffic.

points.title: Schwabentor
points.description: Eastern city gate and surveillance vantage point.

points.title: Tailor Workshop
points.description: Disguise and costume workshop tied to lead progression.

points.title: Lowen Apotheke
points.description: Pharmacy near the cathedral with chemical evidence links.

points.title: Zum Schlappen
points.description: Tavern near Martinstor with working-class witnesses.

points.title: Telegraph Office
points.description: Message relay and switchboard narrative checkpoint.

point.title: Street Raid Lead
point.description: A whispered lead points to a raid already unfolding near the rail yards.

stages.title: Initial Briefing
stages.objectiveHint: Meet Kessler at the bank and open the ledger trail.

stages.title: Cross-Check Leads
stages.objectiveHint: Revisit the bank after checking external testimonies.

stages.title: Open Dog Lead Board
stages.objectiveHint: Collect initial route intel from Rathaus.

stages.title: Converge Leads
stages.objectiveHint: Convene in the park after enough witness routes.

stages.title: Estate Survey
stages.objectiveHint: Open the ghost investigation from the workers' tavern.

stages.title: Assemble Proof
stages.objectiveHint: Correlate bookshelf and floor evidence before accusation.

stages.title: Case Closed
stages.objectiveHint: Ghost case finalized and written into records.

services.label: Rail Yard Whispers
services.baseAccess: Available once the Workers' Pub lead is active and Anna's channels trust you.
services.qualityNote: Fast rumor triage through station messengers and tavern staff.
services.consequenceNote: Loose handling can burn a source and push the network underground.

services.label: Student House Introduction
services.baseAccess: Requires a verified rumor chain plus either one favor in reserve or agency standing.
services.costNote: Usually costs one favor unless your standing is already carrying the weight.
services.qualityNote: Turns a closed fraternity house into a supported Freiburg route instead of a blind trespass.
services.consequenceNote: Commits part of Anna's network to your banker file and is tracked as agency service work.

services.label: Archive Packet
services.baseAccess: Used from Freiburg civic contacts once the Rathaus route is already warm.
services.qualityNote: Provides cross-indexed filing movement notes for follow-up checks.

services.label: Hartmann Salon Introduction
services.baseAccess: Available when Eleonora considers the detective a working contact. Requires at least one completed favor or demonstrated discretion.
services.costNote: No explicit price. Eleonora remembers every door she opens and expects the detective to remember too.
services.qualityNote: Opens house_of_pledges-adjacent events: patronage circles, archival permissions held by aristocratic families, and salon introductions.
services.consequenceNote: Creates implicit obligation. Eleonora tracks debts as social architecture, not accounting.

services.label: Political Cover
services.baseAccess: Available only when active evidence threatens Hartmann family interests or house_of_pledges standing. Case02 and beyond.
services.costNote: Suppression, redirection, or reframing of damaging material. Price: case integrity, moral stress, and Felix trust if he discovers the arrangement.
services.qualityNote: Neutralizes institutional fallout from evidence that would damage Hartmann-adjacent networks.
services.consequenceNote: Accepting cover compromises investigative independence. Refusing it damages Eleonora relations and may close house_of_pledges access.

services.label: Switchboard Trace
services.baseAccess: Available once Lotte trusts the detective enough to share operational patterns. Requires lotte_warning_heeded or equivalent trust threshold.
services.qualityNote: Pattern analysis of telephone traffic reveals communication anomalies around suspects — who called whom, which lines went quiet, which offices redirected.
services.consequenceNote: Using switchboard intelligence risks exposing Lotte's access. Careless handling may burn her position.

services.label: Legal Analysis
services.baseAccess: Available while Felix is an active partner with stable or higher trust. Degrades or becomes unavailable during apathy episodes.
services.qualityNote: Cross-references legal precedent, procedural requirements, and institutional jurisdiction. Turns bureaucratic obstacles into investigative leverage.
services.consequenceNote: Quality depends on Felix mental state. Instrumentalization without reciprocal investment degrades output over time.
